docs.text: Home ¶ Important Links: | Website | Support Server | Patreon | Understanding how to use commands Do not literally type out < > [ ] | ... These are just to display information about the arguments. Some examples of these in use would be: !rps|rockpaperscissors <choice> then both of these will execute the command. !rps <choice> and !rockpaperscissors <choice> To use the Rock, Paper, Scissors command you would type this: !rps rock Understanding command arguments If you see a command with an argument ending with ... this means that this one argument will accept multiple of the same type of argument one after another. Let's use this kick command for example. !kick <members>... <reason> If you want to kick more than one user for the same reason then you would type it like this. !kick @someone @someoneelse They were annoying me For this command, you could also use the users' IDs if you don't wish to mention the users. If you are not sure how to get a users ID please check out this article on Where can I find my User/Server/Message ID . For example: !kick 123456789 987654321 An argument wrapped with [ or ] just means that the argument is optional. For example with the Global required permissions ¶ Friday requires some permissions for all commands. These include: send_messages read_messages embed_links add_reactions How to commands ¶ Prefix ¶ The default prefix for Friday's commands is ! , and for commands to execute every command needs this prefix or the custom prefix that you can set with the prefix command. Slash commands ¶ Friday also supports slash commands. To use these commands, go to any text channel and start typing / , you should be greeted with a list of commands from bots and built-in commands.
docs.title: Home

If you are not sure how to get a users ID please check out this article on Where can I find my User/Server/Message ID . docs.text: FAQ ¶ Why is Friday not responding to me ¶ Friday will respond to @mentions, if you reply to a message from Friday or the channel is set to be a chat channel with !chatchannel . If Friday still doesn't respond then please let me know in the #issues channel in the support server Why is Friday repeating herself? ¶ Because Friday "thinks"/generates her own responses I am not currently sure why this is happening (lol). The current workaround is to use the !reset command, which resets her chat history How to use the bot? ¶ For commands, they are all listed if you use the command !help or on https://docs.friday-bot.com . For chat just mention friday and she will respond to your message How do I make a channel where I don't need to mention the bot to chat? ¶ The chatchannel command is what you're looking for. !chatchannel #channel and more information on https://docs.friday-bot.com/commands/chat/#chatchannel .
docs.title: FAQ

docs.text: Friday will respond to @mentions, if you reply to a message from Friday or the channel is set to be a chat channel with !chatchannel . If Friday still doesn't respond then please let me know in the #issues channel in the support server
docs.title: Why is Friday not responding to me

docs.text: Because Friday "thinks"/generates her own responses I am not currently sure why this is happening (lol). The current workaround is to use the !reset command, which resets her chat history
docs.title: Why is Friday repeating herself?

docs.text: For commands, they are all listed if you use the command !help or on https://docs.friday-bot.com . For chat just mention friday and she will respond to your message
docs.title: How to use the bot?

docs.text: The chatchannel command is what you're looking for. !chatchannel #channel and more information on https://docs.friday-bot.com/commands/chat/#chatchannel .
docs.title: How do I make a channel where I don't need to mention the bot to chat?

docs.text: Chatting with Friday ¶ Friday is a chatbot so these are the ways to make sure that if Friday has a response that you get that response Mentioning Friday (or using a reply) eg. @Friday hey, how are you? Setting a channel to be the chat channel . Friday will respond to every message in that channel as if they were directed to Friday. Eg: hey, what is it like being a bot? Max message length ¶ By default, Friday has a maximum message length that she will respond to. For free users, this is set to 100 characters . To get longer message length become a patreon Rate limiting ¶ Friday has a rate limit per user to help reduce spam and keep costs low. The absolute maximum rate limits are not under my control on what they are. Currently, the rate limits are: 30 messages every 12 hours per user for user that have not voted for Friday and not at least a tier 1 Patreon . 60 messages every 12 hours per user for users that have voted . 100 messages every 12 hours per user for members or guilds that are at least a tier 1 Patreon . 6 messages every 20 seconds per user absolute maximum. 180 messages every 1 hour per user absolute maximum. Using a chat channel ¶ If your server has a set chat channel using the !chatchannel #channel command. You do not need to mention Friday in that channel to talk to her. She will respond to messages without being mentioned. Languages ¶ By default, Friday can understand and speak a lot of languages, whether Friday actually will do this is not up to me unless you use the !language [langauge] command. If you have changes Friday's language to something other than English with the language command, Friday will speak two languages in the server that you used the command in. One of the languages will be the one that you chose and the other will be English. Personas ¶ Personas are only available to patrons. Friday currently has two personas to choose from: Pirate, where Friday will talk like a pirate, and default where Friday is focused on having a good time chatting to everyone. More personas are planned and in-progress.
docs.title: Chat

docs.text: Friday is a chatbot so these are the ways to make sure that if Friday has a response that you get that response Mentioning Friday (or using a reply) eg. @Friday hey, how are you? Setting a channel to be the chat channel . Friday will respond to every message in that channel as if they were directed to Friday. Eg: hey, what is it like being a bot?

docs.text: Automod ¶ There are somethings in this world that bots are better at then humans. Let Friday help out with the moderation of your Discord server. Blacklist ¶ Blacklist words from being sent in text channels Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !blacklist|bl !blacklist|bl add <phrase> !blacklist|bl clear !blacklist|bl display !blacklist|bl punishment <punishments> !blacklist|bl remove <word> bl Add ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Add a word to the current servers blacklist settings. Usage Aliases Examples !blacklist add|+ <phrase> + !blacklist add penis !bl + shit Clear ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Remove all words from the current servers blacklist settings. Usage !blacklist clear Display ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Display the current servers blacklist settings. Usage Aliases !blacklist display|list|show list,show Punishment ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Set the punishment for blacklisted words. Combining kick,ban and/or mute will only apply one of them. Usage Aliases Examples !blacklist punishment|punishments <punishments> punishments !blacklist punishment delete !bl punishments kick !blacklist punishment ban !bl punishments mute !blacklist punishment timeout Remove ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Remove a word from the current servers blacklist settings. Usage Aliases Examples !blacklist remove|- <word> - !blacklist remove penis !bl - shit Contentspam ¶ Sets the max number of message that can have the same content (ignoring who sent the message) until passing the given threshold and muting anyone spamming the same content further. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !contentspam <message_rate> <seconds> !contentspam disable !contentspam punishment <punishments> !contentspam 3 5 !contentspam 15 17 Disable ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Disable the max amount of messages per x seconds with the same content for this server. Usage !contentspam disable Punishment ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Set the punishment for the max amount of message every x seconds. Combining kick,ban and/or mute will only apply one of them. Usage Aliases Examples !contentspam punishment|punishments <punishments> punishments !contentspam punishment delete !contentspam punishments kick !contentspam punishment ban !contentspam punishments mute Invitespam ¶ Automaticaly remove Discord invites (originating from external servers) from text channels. Not giving an argument will display the current setting. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !invitespam|removeinvites [enable] removeinvites !invitespam !removeinvites 1 !invitespam 0 !removeinvites true !invitespam false Mentionspam ¶ Set the max amount of mentions one user can send per message before muting the author Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !mentionspam|maxmentions|maxpings <mention_count> <seconds> !mentionspam|maxmentions|maxpings disable !mentionspam|maxmentions|maxpings punishment <punishments> maxmentions,maxpings !mentionspam 3 !maxmentions 5 !maxpings 10 Disable ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Disable the max amount of mentions per message for this server. Usage !mentionspam disable Punishment ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Set the punishment for the max amount of mentions one user can send per message. Combining kick,ban and/or mute will only apply one of them. Usage Aliases Examples !mentionspam punishment|punishments <punishments> punishments !mentionspam punishment delete !maxmentions punishments kick !maxpings punishment ban !mentionspam punishments mute Messagespam ¶ Sets a max message count for users per x seconds Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !messagespam|maxmessages|ratelimit <message_rate> <seconds> !messagespam|maxmessages|ratelimit disable !messagespam|maxmessages|ratelimit punishment <punishments> maxmessages,ratelimit !messagespam 3 5 !maxmessages 10 12 Disable ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Disable the max amount of messages per x seconds by the same member for this server. Usage !messagespam disable Punishment ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Set the punishment for the max amount of message every x seconds. Combining kick,ban and/or mute will only apply one of them. Usage Aliases Examples !messagespam punishment|punishments <punishments> punishments !messagespam punishment delete !maxmessages punishments kick !ratelimit punishment ban !messagespam punishments mute Unwhitelist ¶ Unwhitelist channels and/or roles from being automoded. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !unwhitelist|unwl <channel_or_roles>... unwl !unwhitelist #memes !unwl @admin #admin 707457407512739951 Whitelist ¶ Whitelist channels and/or roles from being automoded. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !whitelist|wl <channel_or_roles>... wl !whitelist #memes !wl #admin @admin 707457407512739951
docs.title: Automod

docs.text: Config ¶ Botchannel ¶ The channel where bot commands live. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !botchannel <channel> !botchannel clear !botchannel #botspam Clear ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !botchannel clear Disable ¶ Disable a command Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !disable|disablecmd <command> !disable|disablecmd list disablecmd !disable ping !disablecmd ping last !disable "blacklist add" ping List ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Lists all disabled commands. Usage !disable list Enable ¶ Enables the selected command(s). Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !enable <command> Prefix ¶ Sets the prefix for Fridays commands Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !prefix <new_prefix> !prefix ? !prefix f! Restrict ¶ Restricts the selected command to the bot channel. Ignored with manage server permission. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !restrict <command> !restrict list List ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !restrict list Serverlanguage ¶ Change the language that I will speak in a server. For application commands, this will be overridden by your client settings. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !serverlanguage|serverlang|guildlang|guildlanguage <language> serverlang,guildlang,guildlanguage !serverlanguage en !serverlang es !guildlang english !guildlanguage Español Unrestrict ¶ Unrestricts the selected command. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !unrestrict <command> Updates ¶ Recieve updates on new features and changes for Friday Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !updates <channel> Userlanguage ¶ Change the language that I will speak to you as a user. For application commands, this will be overridden by your client settings. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !userlanguage|userlang <language> userlang !userlanguage en !userlang es !userlanguage english !userlang Español

docs.text: Fun ¶ Fun games and other commands to give more life to your Discord server. 8ball ¶ Ask the magic 8ball a question Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !8ball <question> Choice ¶ Pick a random item from a list. For multiple items, separate them with a comma. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !choice|pick|select <choices> pick,select Coinflip ¶ Flip a coin Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !coinflip|coin coin Countdown ¶ Start a countdown. This command only updates every 10 seconds to avoid being ratelimited by Discord Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !countdown|cd [hours=0] [minutes=0] [seconds=0] [title] cd Minesweeper ¶ Play minesweeper Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !minesweeper|ms [size=5] [bomb_count=6] ms !minesweeper !ms 9 10 !minesweeper 5 5 Poll ¶ Make a poll. Contain each option in qoutes 'option' 'option 2' Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !poll <title> <option1> <option2> <option3> <option4> <option5> <option6> <option7> <option8> <option9> <option10> !poll "this is a title" '1' '2' '3' !poll "Do you like being pinged for random things" Yes No "I just mute everything" Rng ¶ Get a random number between the given range Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !rng <start> <end> Rockpaperscissors ¶ Play Rock Paper Scissors with Friday Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !rockpaperscissors|rps <rock, paper or scissors> rps Souptime ¶ Soup Time Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !souptime

docs.text: Logging ¶ Different from the log cog b/c this one deals with discord logging not bot logging Modlog ¶ Set the channel where I can log moderation actions. This will log moderation action done by Friday, Fridays commands, and other moderation action logged by Discord. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !modlog|modlogs [channel] !modlog|modlogs events <events>... modlogs Events ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands The events that will be logged in the mod log channel Usage Examples !modlog events <events>... !modlog events bans !modlogs events mutes !modlog events unbans !modlogs events unmutes !modlog events kicks !modlogs events bans mutes kicks unbans unmutes !modlog events bans unbans

docs.text: Moderation ¶ Manage your server with these commands Ban ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !ban [duration] <members>... [reason] !ban !ban 20m @username @someone @someoneelse Spam !ban @thisguy The most spam i have ever seen !ban 12345678910 10987654321 @someone !ban @someone They were annoying me !ban 40d 123456789 2 Sus Begone ¶ Delete unwanted message that I send Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !begone [message] !begone !begone https://discord.com/channels/707441352367013899/707458929696702525/707520808448294983 !begone 707520808448294983 Kick ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !kick <members>... [reason] !kick @username @someone @someoneelse !kick @thisguy !kick 12345678910 10987654321 @someone !kick @someone I just really didn't like them !kick @thisguy 12345678910 They were spamming general Language ¶ Change the language that I will speak. This currently only applies to the chatbot messages not the commands. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !language|lang [language] lang !language !lang en !language es !lang english !language spanish Last ¶ Gets the last member to leave a voice channel. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !last [voice_channel] Massmove ¶ Move everyone from one voice channel to another Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !massmove|move <to_channel> [from_channel] move !massmove general !move vc-2 general !massmove 'long voice channel' general Mute ¶ Mute a member from text channels Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !mute [duration] <members>... [reason] !mute role [role] !mute !mute 20m @Motostar @steve they were annoying me !mute @steve 9876543210 !mute 30d @Motostar spamming general !mute 0123456789 Role ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Set the role to be applied to members that get muted Usage !mute role [role] !mute role create [name='Muted'] !mute role update Rolecall ¶ Moves everyone with a specific role to a voicechannel. Objects that can be exluded are voicechannels,roles,and members Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !rolecall|rc <role> [voicechannel] <exclusions>... rc !rolecall @mods vc-1 !rc 123456798910 vc-2 vc-1 10987654321 !rolecall @admins general @username @username Selfmute ¶ Temporarily mutes yourself for the specified duration, min 5 minutes, max 24 hours Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !selfmute <duration> !selfmute 20m !selfmute 1h20m !selfmute 5h Selftimeout ¶ Temporarily timeout yourself for the specified duration, min 5 minutes, max 24 hours Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !selftimeout <duration> !selftimeout 20m !selftimeout 1h20m !selftimeout 5h Timeout ¶ Timeout a member from chating, replying, reacting, and joining voice channels. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !timeout <duration> <members>... [reason] !timeout 20m @Motostar @steve they were annoying me !timeout 1week @steve 9876543210 !timeout 5d @Motostar spamming general !timeout 0123456789 Unban ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !unban <member> <reason> Unmute ¶ Unmute a member from text channels Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !unmute <members>... <reason> !unmute @Motostar @steve they said sorry !unmute @steve 9876543210 !unmute @Motostar !unmute 0123456789 Untimeout ¶ Remove the timeout of a member Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !untimeout|removetimeout <members>... <reason> removetimeout !untimeout @Motostar @steve they said sorry !removetimeout @steve 9876543210 !untimeout @Motostar !removetimeout 0123456789
docs.title: Moderation

docs.text: Music ¶ Listen to your favourite music and audio clips with Friday's music commands Connect ¶ Join a voice channel Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !connect|join [channel] join Custom ¶ Play sounds/songs without looking for the url everytime Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !custom|c <name> !custom|c add <name> <url> !custom|c change <name> <url> !custom|c clear !custom|c list !custom|c remove <name> c Add ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !custom add <name> <url> Change ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !custom change|replace <name> <url> replace Clear ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !custom clear List ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !custom list Remove ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !custom remove|del <name> del Equalizer ¶ Change the players equalizer. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !equalizer|eq <equalizer> eq !equalizer flat !eq boost !equalizer metal !eq piano Nowplaying ¶ Update the player controller. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !nowplaying|np|now_playing|current np,now_playing,current Pause ¶ Pause the currently playing song. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !pause Play ¶ Follow this command with the title of a song to search for it or just paste the Youtube/SoundCloud url if the search gives and undesirable result Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !play|p|add <url/title> p,add !play https://youtu.be/dQw4w9WgXcQ Queue ¶ shows the song queue Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !queue|que !queue|que remove <index> que Remove ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Remove an item from the queue Usage Aliases !queue remove|rm <index> rm Resume ¶ Resume a currently paused player. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !resume Shuffle ¶ Shuffle the players queue. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !shuffle|mix mix Skip ¶ Skips the current song Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !skip Stop ¶ Stops the currently playing music Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !stop|disconnect disconnect Swap_dj ¶ Swap who has control over the music. (Admins always have control) Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !swap_dj|swap <member> swap Volume ¶ Change the players volume, between 1 and 100. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases !volume|v|vol <vol> v,vol

docs.text: Redditlink ¶ Extract the media from Reddit posts with Friday's Reddit command and more. Friday has a feature that will extract Reddit media. To use this feature simply paste the link to a Reddit post in a text channel and Friday will react with 🔗 if media is extractable. To extract the media just react with 🔗 as well, and Friday will begin the process. Redditextract ¶ Extracts the media from the reddit post Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !redditextract <link> !redditextract enable <enable> Enable ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Enable or disabled Friday's reddit link extraction. (When disabled Friday won't react to reddit links.) Usage !redditextract enable <enable>
docs.title: Redditlinks

To extract the media just react with 🔗 as well, and Friday will begin the process.

docs.text: Reminder ¶ Reminder ¶ Create a reminder for a certain time in the future. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Aliases Examples !reminder|timer|remind <when> <message> !reminder|timer|remind clear !reminder|timer|remind delete <id> !reminder|timer|remind list timer,remind !reminder 20m go buy food !timer do something in 20m !remind jan 1st happy new years Clear ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Delete all your reminders. Usage !reminder clear Delete ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Delete a reminder. Usage Aliases Examples !reminder delete|remove|cancel <id> remove,cancel !reminder delete 1 !timer remove 200 List ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands List all reminders. Usage !reminder list

docs.text: Stars ¶ A starboard to upvote posts obviously. There are two ways to make use of this feature, the first is via reactions, react to a message with ⭐ and the bot will automatically add (or remove) it to the starboard. The second way is via Developer Mode. Enable it under Settings > Appearance > Developer Mode and then you get access to Copy ID and using the star/unstar commands. Star ¶ Stars a message via message ID. To star a message you should right click on the on a message and then click "Copy ID". You must have Developer Mode enabled to get that functionality. It is recommended that you react to a message with ⭐ instead. You can only star a message once. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !star <message> !star clean <stars> !star limit <stars> !star lock !star migrate !star random !star show <message> !star stats [member] !star unlock !star 707520808448294983 !star https://discord.com/channels/707441352367013899/707458929696702525/707520808448294983 Clean ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Cleans the starboard This removes messages in the starboard that only have less than or equal to the number of specified stars. This defaults to 1. Note that this only checks the last 100 messages in the starboard. This command requires the Manage Server permission. Usage Examples !star clean <stars> !star clean 1 !star clean 3 Limit ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Sets the minimum number of stars required to show up. When this limit is set, messages must have this number or more to show up in the starboard channel. You cannot have a negative number and the maximum star limit you can set is 100. Note that messages that previously did not meet the limit but now do will still not show up in the starboard until starred again. You must have Manage Server permissions to use this. Usage Aliases Examples !star limit|threshold <stars> threshold !star limit 3 !star threshold 5 Lock ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Locks the starboard from being processed. This is a moderation tool that allows you to temporarily disable the starboard to aid in dealing with star spam. When the starboard is locked, no new entries are added to the starboard as the bot will no longer listen to reactions or star/unstar commands. To unlock the starboard, use the unlock subcommand. To use this command you need Manage Server permission. Usage !star lock Migrate ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Migrates the starboard to the newest version. While doing this, the starboard is locked. Note: This is an incredibly expensive operation . It will take a very long time. You must have Manage Server permissions to use this. Usage !star migrate Random ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Shows a random starred message. Usage !star random Show ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Shows a starred message via its ID. To get the ID of a message you should right click on the message and then click "Copy ID". You must have Developer Mode enabled to get that functionality. You can only use this command once per 10 seconds. Usage Examples !star show <message> !star show 707520808448294983 !star show https://discord.com/channels/707441352367013899/707458929696702525/707520808448294983 Stats ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Shows statistics on the starboard usage of the server or a member. Usage Examples !star stats [member] !star stats !star stats 215227961048170496 !star stats @Motostar Unlock ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Unlocks the starboard for re-processing. To use this command you need Manage Server permission. Usage !star unlock Starboard ¶ Sets up the starboard for this server. This creates a new channel with the specified name and makes it into the server's "starboard". If no name is passed in then it defaults to "starboard". Choose wisely, this channel cannot be changed. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !starboard [name='starboard'] !starboard info !starboard !starboard starboard Info ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Shows meta information about the starboard. Usage !starboard info Unstar ¶ Unstars a message via message ID. To unstar a message you should right click on the on a message and then click "Copy ID". You must have Developer Mode enabled to get that functionality. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !unstar <message> !unstar 707520808448294983 !unstar https://discord.com/channels/707441352367013899/707458929696702525/707520808448294983
docs.title: Starboard

docs.text: Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Cleans the starboard This removes messages in the starboard that only have less than or equal to the number of specified stars. This defaults to 1. Note that this only checks the last 100 messages in the starboard. This command requires the Manage Server permission. Usage Examples !star clean <stars> !star clean 1 !star clean 3
docs.title: Clean

docs.text: Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Sets the minimum number of stars required to show up. When this limit is set, messages must have this number or more to show up in the starboard channel. You cannot have a negative number and the maximum star limit you can set is 100. Note that messages that previously did not meet the limit but now do will still not show up in the starboard until starred again. You must have Manage Server permissions to use this. Usage Aliases Examples !star limit|threshold <stars> threshold !star limit 3 !star threshold 5
docs.title: Limit

docs.text: Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Locks the starboard from being processed. This is a moderation tool that allows you to temporarily disable the starboard to aid in dealing with star spam. When the starboard is locked, no new entries are added to the starboard as the bot will no longer listen to reactions or star/unstar commands. To unlock the starboard, use the unlock subcommand. To use this command you need Manage Server permission. Usage !star lock
docs.title: Lock

docs.text: Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Migrates the starboard to the newest version. While doing this, the starboard is locked. Note: This is an incredibly expensive operation . It will take a very long time. You must have Manage Server permissions to use this. Usage !star migrate
docs.title: Migrate

docs.text: Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Shows a random starred message. Usage !star random
docs.title: Random

docs.text: Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Shows a starred message via its ID. To get the ID of a message you should right click on the message and then click "Copy ID". You must have Developer Mode enabled to get that functionality. You can only use this command once per 10 seconds. Usage Examples !star show <message> !star show 707520808448294983 !star show https://discord.com/channels/707441352367013899/707458929696702525/707520808448294983
docs.title: Show

docs.text: Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Shows statistics on the starboard usage of the server or a member. Usage Examples !star stats [member] !star stats !star stats 215227961048170496 !star stats @Motostar
docs.title: Stats

docs.text: Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Unlocks the starboard for re-processing. To use this command you need Manage Server permission. Usage !star unlock
docs.title: Unlock

docs.text: Sets up the starboard for this server. This creates a new channel with the specified name and makes it into the server's "starboard". If no name is passed in then it defaults to "starboard". Choose wisely, this channel cannot be changed. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !starboard [name='starboard'] !starboard info !starboard !starboard starboard
docs.title: Starboard

docs.text: Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Shows meta information about the starboard. Usage !starboard info
docs.title: Info

docs.text: Unstars a message via message ID. To unstar a message you should right click on the on a message and then click "Copy ID". You must have Developer Mode enabled to get that functionality. Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage Examples !unstar <message> !unstar 707520808448294983 !unstar https://discord.com/channels/707441352367013899/707458929696702525/707520808448294983
docs.title: Unstar

docs.text: Welcome ¶ Welcome ¶ Friday's settings for welcomeing new members to your servers Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !welcome !welcome channel [channel] !welcome display !welcome message [message] !welcome role [role] Channel ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Setup a welcome channel for Friday to welcome new memebers in Usage Examples !welcome channel [channel] !welcome channel !welcome channel #welcome !welcome channel #general !welcome channel 707458929696702525 Display ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Shows the servers current welcome settings Usage Aliases !welcome display|list|show list,show Message ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Set a message to greet new members to your server, message variables are {user} , {server} Usage Examples !welcome message [message] !welcome message !welcome message Welcome to the server {user}, stay a while! !welcome message Welcome {user} to {server} !welcome message A new member has joined the server! Role ¶ Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Set the role that is given to new members when they join the server Usage Examples !welcome role [role] !welcome role !welcome role @default !welcome role 12345678910
docs.title: Welcome

docs.text: Friday's settings for welcomeing new members to your servers Does not have a slash command to match Learn more about slash commands Usage !welcome !welcome channel [channel] !welcome display !welcome message [message] !welcome role [role]
docs.title: Welcome

docs.title: Channel

docs.title: Display

docs.title: Message

docs.title: Role

docs.text: Activate Friday's perks ¶ Activate a server ¶ So you have become a Patreon supporter and would like to receive your benefits. To activate Friday on your server of choosing simply go to that server and use the !patreon server true command. If you were to choose to deactivate please use the !patreon server false command. To make sure that this command works in the server that you choose, double check the following. You are in the "Friday Development" Discord server You have the Supporting on Patreon role You have the Proper role for your Patreon Tier. The roles will be named something like. One server Patreons Three server Patreons Tier 2 One server Patreons Tier 2 Three server Patreons If you have checked all of the above and Friday is still not activating, please let me know in the #issues channel of the support server. Activate for yourself ¶ Note There is nothing extra you need to do for Fridays user perks to work. Any chat that you can communicate with Friday will have your perks.
docs.title: Activate Friday's perks

docs.text: So you have become a Patreon supporter and would like to receive your benefits. To activate Friday on your server of choosing simply go to that server and use the !patreon server true command. If you were to choose to deactivate please use the !patreon server false command. To make sure that this command works in the server that you choose, double check the following. You are in the "Friday Development" Discord server You have the Supporting on Patreon role You have the Proper role for your Patreon Tier. The roles will be named something like. One server Patreons Three server Patreons Tier 2 One server Patreons Tier 2 Three server Patreons If you have checked all of the above and Friday is still not activating, please let me know in the #issues channel of the support server.
docs.title: Activate a server

docs.text: Note There is nothing extra you need to do for Fridays user perks to work. Any chat that you can communicate with Friday will have your perks.
docs.title: Activate for yourself

docs.text: Voting ¶ Voting for Friday on Top.gg really helps with getting Friday to more people because the more votes a bot has the higher ranking it gets. To get Friday higher in the rankings you can vote here top.gg/bot/476303446547365891/vote What do I get for voting ¶ When voting you will receive some cool perks currently including: Better rate limits when chatting with Friday as displayed here
docs.title: Voting
